positions.name: iShares Core S&P 500 UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Core broad market exposure to S&P 500. IE-domiciled accumulating structure eliminates 30% WHT on distributions — structurally optimal vehicle for a Lebanese resident. Portfolio anchor targeting ~36% of holdings NAV. APRIL 22 (WEEK 2) UPDATE: Position at 34.10% of holdings NAV (1.8025 shares, $1,373.54 market value at $762.02/share, +6.21% unrealized). Gap to 36% target: approximately $76-83 on holdings NAV basis. ASML exit proceeds ($150-170) to be deployed here immediately on ASML exit — closes and slightly exceeds the 36% target. S&P 500 at 7,064 (April 21 close); April 20-21 pullback (-0.24%, -0.63%) driven by Iran ceasefire uncertainty. IT sector at 31.9% of S&P 500 weight, recovering strongly as sector rotation out of Energy and into Technology accelerates. $142B AUM, 0.07% TER. Thesis conviction: STRONGER. No structural invalidation conditions triggered.
invalidation_conditions: Structural shift away from US equities as the dominant long-term growth engine
A superior WHT-efficient broad market vehicle becomes available

positions.name: Amazon.com Inc
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy entered during Liberation Day tariff shock. Core thesis: AWS cloud dominance (~33% global cloud market share), advertising revenue at double-digit growth, and AI infrastructure investment (Bedrock, Trainium custom silicon). APRIL 22 (WEEK 2) UPDATE: Current price $249.91 (+16.83% unrealized from $213.94 cost basis). BofA raised target to $298, KeyBanc to $325, TD Cowen reiterating $300. Average target $286.23 (+15.3% upside). AWS growth estimated at 28% YoY for Q1 2026 with $1.3B Anthropic contribution; BofA above Street at 28% vs consensus 25%. Q1 2026 earnings April 29, 2026 at 5:30 PM ET. Street consensus: $1.63 EPS, $177-178B revenue, $20.7B EBIT. 42 analyst Buys vs 3 Holds. RSI elevated — no pre-earnings add warranted. Review ADD $50-75 if April 29 results show AWS >25% AND EBIT above $20B AND Q2 guidance maintained or raised. Thesis conviction: STRONGER.
invalidation_conditions: AWS loses meaningful cloud market share to Azure or GCP on a sustained basis
Regulatory breakup that separates AWS from the retail/advertising business
Sustained margin compression across all segments (retail, advertising, AND cloud simultaneously)
Q1 2026 operating income below the low end of the $16.5B guidance floor with a Q2 guide-down

positions.name: SPDR Gold Shares
positions.thesis: Macro hedge against dollar weakness, geopolitical risk, and inflationary pressure. Gold has no cash flows so carries zero WHT exposure despite US domicile. APRIL 22 (WEEK 2) UPDATE — WATCH FLAG ADDED: GLD at $429.57 (April 21 close), DOWN from $442.09 (April 20 close) — a -2.83% single-day decline that narrows the buffer above the $420 trim trigger to only 2.3% (down from 5.3% in prior report). Daily close alert at $420 required. Despite price softening, all formal macro thesis pillars remain intact: Iran parliament drafting toll/blocking law for hostile vessels at Hormuz; ceasefire extended by Trump citing Iran's 'fractured government' (April 22); nuclear talks fully collapsed; Hormuz effectively still closed. Spot gold ~$4,810-$4,830/oz. Institutional targets UPGRADED: Goldman Sachs $5,400 year-end (reaffirmed); JPMorgan $6,000-$6,300 year-end; Wells Fargo $6,100-$6,300 year-end; Deutsche Bank $6,000 year-end. Central bank demand: ~850 tonnes forecast 2026, PBOC continuing reserve building. 4-week risk-on invalidation clock NOT started — ceasefire extension without formal peace deal does not restart clock. Thesis conviction: SAME (macro pillars intact; price proximity to $420 trigger elevates to WATCH).
invalidation_conditions: Sustained USD strengthening cycle driven by structurally higher real rates that persist above inflation
Major reversal in central bank gold buying trend (multiple major central banks becoming net sellers)
GLD breaks and holds below $420 — triggers 25% trim rebalance into CSPX

Iran war fully and durably resolved AND Strait of Hormuz formally reopened AND risk-on environment maintained for 4+ consecutive weeks — assess 25% trim at that point (4-week clock fully reset as of April 18-19 Hormuz re-closure; ceasefire expiry April 23 does NOT restart the clock without a formal peace deal)

positions.name: Mastercard Inc
positions.wht_exposure: 30% WHT on dividend — confirmed $3.33 withheld YTD (MA dividend only). Annualised WHT drag <$4 at current position size. Immaterial.
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy during Liberation Day volatility. Thesis: payments network duopoly (MA + Visa) with near-monopoly economics, asset-light model generating high ROE, global consumer spending volume exposure, and pricing power. APRIL 22 (WEEK 2) UPDATE: Current price $511.35 (-0.37% unrealized from $513.24 cost basis). 29 Strong Buy / 4 Moderate Buy / 4 Hold; average price target $652.66 (+25.2% upside from current). Earnings EPS estimate $4.40/share (+18% YoY from $3.73). Q1 2026 earnings confirmed April 30, 2026. Q1 covers January-March (pre-peak Iran escalation), so volumes expected clean. Q1 2026 guidance: net revenue growth at low end of low-double digits on a currency-neutral basis (FX tailwind +3.5-4.0pp). Market is trading MA in the $520-$600 range while awaiting Q1 volume confirmation — current $511.35 is below that floor, suggesting slight undervaluation. Key watch metrics for ADD $100 on April 30: cross-border volume must exceed Q4 2025's 14% rate AND VAS growth must sustain above 20% (Q4 exit rate 22%) AND guidance maintained or raised. Stagflation risk from Iran/Hormuz is a medium-term headwind to cross-border but unlikely to show in Q1 data. WHT drag <$4/year at current size — immaterial. Thesis conviction: SAME (pending April 30 data).
invalidation_conditions: Disintermediation by real-time payment rails (FedNow, open banking) gaining scale at the expense of card networks
Regulatory caps on interchange fees in core US or European markets
Q1 2026 earnings miss on cross-border volumes or management guides down — re-evaluate immediately on April 30
Sustained global consumer spending contraction lasting multiple quarters

positions.name: NVIDIA Corporation
positions.thesis: AI infrastructure buildout driving unprecedented GPU demand. NVDA holds dominant position in AI training and inference hardware; CUDA ecosystem creates deep switching costs. APRIL 22 (WEEK 2) UPDATE: Current price $199.88 (+9.84% unrealized from $181.98 cost basis) — slightly below prior report's $202 'elevated' level, modest improvement in entry dynamics. Vera Rubin confirmed in full production: 3.3x speed improvement over Blackwell Ultra, 4x efficiency gains, H2 2026 shipments on track. Jensen Huang reaffirmed '$1 trillion+' combined Blackwell AND Vera Rubin backlog through 2027. NEW RISK IDENTIFIED: Projected 500,000 unit shortfall in Rubin GPU production due to HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) supply constraints — near-term production headwind but not a thesis invalidation. Monitor at May 20 Q1 FY2027 earnings. Q1 FY2027 consensus: $78.8B revenue, $1.76 EPS. Analyst targets raised to $275-$360 post-GTC 2026; average target ~$270 (+35% upside). Hyperscalers collectively committing $600B+ in 2026 AI capex. Add target: $180-185 pullback only. Thesis conviction: STRONGEST IN PORTFOLIO.
invalidation_conditions: AMD or custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Microsoft Maia) takes meaningful and sustained share in AI training workloads
AI capex cycle reverses — hyperscalers visibly cut or guide down infrastructure spend across multiple quarters
Export restrictions to China expand materially beyond current guidance, reducing total addressable market by >15%
Post-GTC 2026: $1T order backlog proves overstated or a significant portion of orders cancelled in subsequent quarters
Vera Rubin production delays extending beyond Q3 2026 — would defer the next re-rating catalyst

positions.name: ASML Holding NV
positions.wht_exposure: 15% Dutch WHT assumed (treaty rate). Verification still outstanding. NOW MOOT — FULL EXIT RECOMMENDED AND MAINTAINED.
positions.thesis: THESIS FLAG: DETERIORATING — FULL EXIT RECOMMENDED. 3RD CONSECUTIVE REPORT AS OF 2026-04-22. OPERATIONALLY OVERDUE. EUV lithography monopoly remains structurally intact (no credible competitor on a 5-10 year horizon). However, ALL material invalidation conditions are simultaneously materializing and re-confirmed this week: (1) China mix collapsed from 36% (Q4 2025) to 19% (Q1 2026) in a single quarter — on a clear trajectory to breach the 15% invalidation threshold in Q2 2026 results (approximately July 2026). (2) MATCH Act (bipartisan DUV bill) advancing in Congress — would cut off ASML DUV sales AND maintenance services to China; JPMorgan warns -10% EPS impact. (3) Stock fell 6% post-Q1 results despite a beat (EUR 8.8B revenue vs EUR 8.5B consensus, EPS EUR 7.15 vs EUR 6.00) and a full-year guidance raise to EUR 36-40B — market is actively pre-pricing the multiple de-rating with no bounce. Current price $1,458.97 (+4.42% unrealized from $1,397.14 cost basis). Market value $253.71. FULL EXIT REQUIRED (sell 0.1739 shares at market). Redeploy $150-170 into CSPX immediately post-exit; reserve $100 for MA April 30 conditional add; retain $50 buffer. Every day this position is held beyond today (April 22) represents execution risk.
invalidation_conditions: China develops a credible domestic EUV alternative — assessed as a 5-10 year horizon risk, not near-term
Semiconductor capex supercycle ends and fab customers cancel or significantly delay EUV orders at scale
Dutch or US government export restrictions expand materially beyond current ASML guidance, causing revenue guidance to be CUT (not merely compressed China mix)
China mix falls below 15% of net sales in Q2 2026 results — APPROACHING THRESHOLD: currently at 19% and declining rapidly (was 36% in Q4 2025)
US DUV restriction bill passes into law — bipartisan bill NOW ACTIVELY ADVANCING in Congress as of April 2026
Additional China export restriction threat beyond existing controls — CONFIRMED materializing April 20, 2026

positions.name: iShares USD Treasury Bond 0-3 Month UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Cash equivalent / capital preservation layer with 0% effective WHT. Replaced SGOV (which carried 30% WHT on distributions) — structurally optimal for Lebanese residents. US Treasury interest paid to an Irish-domiciled fund qualifies for the portfolio interest exemption from NRA withholding. Near-zero duration risk (0-3M). APRIL 22 (WEEK 2) UPDATE: Position at 14.92% of holdings NAV (5.6247 shares at $106.80, $600.72 market value, -1.20% unrealized). YTM CONFIRMED at 3.68% (April 13 BlackRock data) — above the 3.0% trim trigger. NAV Total Return YTD: +1.01% (as of April 13). Fed holding at 3.50-3.75%; FOMC April 28-29 overwhelmingly expected to hold (JPM: 'easy call'). JPMorgan forecasts no Fed cuts in 2026 (possible 25bps hike Q3 2027); bond markets price ~50bps easing. Iran ceasefire extended by Trump (April 22) but Hormuz structurally still closed — maintains near-term capital preservation rationale. STAGE-DOWN PLAN ACTIVE: Target reduction from 14.92% toward ~10% of NAV in stages. April 28-29 FOMC is the next trigger: dovish signal (rate cut window mentioned) = first stage-down of ~$100 redeployed into CSPX immediately. Do not trim before FOMC clarity. Thesis conviction: SAME.
invalidation_conditions: Fed cuts rates aggressively (>100bps in 2026) — opportunity cost vs equities rises significantly, accelerate trim
A better WHT-efficient fixed income vehicle with superior risk-adjusted return is identified
Portfolio NAV grows large enough (>$10K) that maintaining a 15% cash equivalent layer imposes unacceptable drag on long-term returns
YTM falls below 3.0% — at that point, reduce toward 8-10% of NAV immediately
